openmeter/meter/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing meter.Service and meter.ManageService interfaces. Owns all DB reads and writes for meter entities, enforcing namespace isolation and soft-delete semantics via DeletedAt.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every mutating method
patterns.description: Every mutating DB operation wraps its body in transaction.Run + entutils.TransactingRepo (or WithNoValue). Reads-only methods use entutils.TransactingRepo directly without transaction.Run.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context) (meterpkg.Meter, error) { return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, repo *Adapter) (meterpkg.Meter, error) { ... }) })

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad required by TransactingRepo
patterns.description: Adapter implements entutils.TxCreator: Tx() hijacks transaction via HijackTx+NewTxDriver, WithTx() creates txClient from raw config, Self() returns self. Omitting any method breaks TransactingRepo rebinding.
patterns.example: func (a *Adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *Adapter { txClient := db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &Adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all required fields (Client, Logger) before constructing the Adapter; returns error on invalid config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } return &Adapter{db: config.Client, logger: config.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ meter.Service = (*Adapter)(nil) at package level ensures compile-time interface satisfaction. Add similar assertions for any new interface this adapter implements.

patterns.name: MapFromEntityFactory for all domain mapping
patterns.description: All query paths call MapFromEntityFactory to convert *db.Meter to meter.Meter. Never construct domain objects inline from Ent entities.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt filter
patterns.description: Meters are soft-deleted by setting DeletedAt; queries default to filtering DeletedAt IS NULL. Only omit the filter when IncludeDeleted is explicitly set.

patterns.name: Typed error wrapping
patterns.description: db.IsConstraintError -> models.NewGenericConflictError; db.IsNotFound -> meter.NewMeterNotFoundError; other errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf.
patterns.example: if db.IsConstraintError(err) { return meterpkg.Meter{}, models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("meter with the same slug already exists")) }

key_file_guides.role: Core Adapter struct, Config, constructor, and Tx/WithTx/Self triad implementation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting to implement any of Tx/WithTx/Self breaks TransactingRepo for this adapter.

key_file_guides.role: Create/Update/Delete meter + cross-entity checks (HasActiveFeatureForMeter, HasEntitlementForMeter, ListFeaturesForMeter). Uses featureadapter.MapFeatureEntity for feature mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Cross-entity queries use entitlementdb and featuredb predicates; missing namespace Where clauses can leak cross-namespace data.

key_file_guides.role: ListMeters (pagination, filtering, ordering) and GetMeterByIDOrSlug plus MapFromEntityFactory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ordering switch must handle all meter.OrderBy constants or return GenericValidationError; missing cases silently use default ordering.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests using testutils.InitPostgresDB and schema.Create to provision a real schema.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context(); using context.Background() breaks test-scoped cancellation and transaction cleanup.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly in a helper method body without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-carried transaction.
Hand-writing SQL instead of using Ent query builders.
Returning raw Ent entities from public functions instead of mapping through MapFromEntityFactory.
Omitting DeletedAt IS NULL filter in queries where soft-deleted meters should be excluded.

decisions.decision: Adapter implements both meter.Service and meter.ManageService at the DB layer; service/ wraps it for business logic.
decisions.rationale: Keeps persistence concerns isolated; ManageService in service/ layer adds hooks, event publishing, and cross-domain validation without mixing them into SQL.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete via DeletedAt rather than hard DELETE.
decisions.rationale: Meters referenced by historical billing/usage data must not be physically removed to preserve audit trails and FK integrity.

code_examples.scenario: Create a meter inside a transaction using TransactingRepo

openmeter/meter/httphandler.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for the meter domain. Exposes meter CRUD, query (JSON and CSV), subject list, and group-by endpoints using the httptransport.Handler pattern; delegates business logic to meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector.

patterns.name: Handler interface + factory function
patterns.description: Handler interface composes sub-handler interfaces (MeterHandler). New(...) constructs the concrete handler struct; var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) asserts compliance.
patterns.example: func New(namespaceDecoder, customerService, meterService, streaming, subjectService, options...) Handler { return &handler{...} }

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints with path parameters use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params]; the decoder func receives the parsed params alongside *http.Request.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved at decode time
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first and embeds the resolved namespace in the request struct — never passes raw http.Request to service layer.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, err }

patterns.name: Mapping functions live in mapping.go
patterns.description: All api <-> domain type conversions (ToAPIMeter, ToAPIMeterQueryResult, toQueryParamsFromRequest) live in mapping.go; handler files must not inline type conversions.

patterns.name: JSON path validation via streaming.Connector before meter mutations
patterns.description: validateJSONPaths and validateGroupByJSONPaths call streaming.ValidateJSONPath before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter because ClickHouse rejects invalid paths at query time.

patterns.name: CSV response via commonhttp.CSVResponseEncoder
patterns.description: CSV endpoints return commonhttp.CSVResponse (interface with Records() + FileName()). queryMeterCSVResult in query_csv.go implements this interface.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface declaration, concrete handler struct, New constructor with all service dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new endpoint requires extending the MeterHandler interface and implementing the method on *handler.

key_file_guides.role: Domain <-> API type converters and toQueryParamsFromRequest (resolves customer IDs, validates group-by, maps window timezone).
key_file_guides.watch_for: toQueryParamsFromRequest calls customerService.ListCustomers — customer-not-found must surface as GenericNotFoundError, not 500.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD handler implementations (List, Get, Create, Update, Delete) and validateJSONPaths helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateMeter fetches current meter first to resolve ID; removing this fetch breaks the update flow.

key_file_guides.role: QueryMeter (GET), QueryMeterPost (POST), ListSubjects, ListGroupByValues — all delegate to streaming.Connector after resolving the meter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListGroupByValues defaults From to last 24 hours when both From and To are nil — must remain to avoid unbounded ClickHouse scans.

key_file_guides.role: CSV variants of query endpoints; queryMeterCSVResult.Records() builds header/data rows with optional subject display names.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Subject display names are best-effort enrichment; missing subjects fill with empty string, not an error.

anti_patterns: Calling meter.ManageService or streaming.Connector directly in the decoder func — decoding must only extract and validate HTTP input.
Inline type conversions in handler files instead of mapping.go functions.
Skipping validateJSONPaths before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter — ClickHouse will reject invalid JSONPath expressions at query time.
Returning 500 for customer-not-found in filter resolution — must return models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
Using context.Background() instead of propagating ctx from the request.

decisions.decision: handler struct holds both meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector directly.
decisions.rationale: Meter query operations (QueryMeter, ListSubjects, ListGroupByValues) run directly against ClickHouse via Connector, bypassing the meter service to avoid double-hop latency.

decisions.decision: POST body query (QueryMeterPost) is mapped via ToRequestFromQueryParamsPOSTBody before calling toQueryParamsFromRequest.
decisions.rationale: Single shared params-to-streaming conversion path reduces divergence between GET and POST query semantics.

code_examples.scenario: Typical handler method: decode path param + namespace, delegate to service, map response

openmeter/meter/mockadapter.purpose: In-memory mock implementation of meter.Service and meter.ManageService for use in tests. Optionally syncs meters to PostgreSQL via SetDBClient to satisfy FK constraints on features.meter_id.

patterns.name: Two-level struct: adapter (Service) + manageAdapter (ManageService)
patterns.description: adapter implements meter.Service with an in-memory slice; manageAdapter embeds adapter and adds Create/Update/Delete/RegisterPreUpdateMeterHook, satisfying meter.ManageService.

patterns.name: New vs NewManage factory selection
patterns.description: Tests needing read-only access call New(meters); tests needing full mutation access call NewManage(meters). Both validate input meters.

patterns.name: Optional PG sync via SetDBClient
patterns.description: Call adapter.SetDBClient(*entdb.Client) after New to make CreateMeter/ReplaceMeters persist rows to PostgreSQL for FK integrity. PG sync happens before in-memory state updates.

patterns.name: ReplaceMeters for bulk test seeding
patterns.description: ReplaceMeters atomically replaces all in-memory meters and syncs to PG if SetDBClient was called. Reuses existing PG rows by (namespace, key) to handle shared template DBs.

patterns.name: Zero-page pagination returns full dataset
patterns.description: ListMeters returns all matching items when params.Page.IsZero(), skipping offset math — mirrors real pagination behavior for unpaginated callers.
patterns.example: if params.Page.IsZero() { return pagination.Result[meter.Meter]{Items: meters, TotalCount: len(meters)}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Core in-memory adapter struct, New/NewManage constructors, SetDBClient, TestAdapter type alias.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestAdapter type alias exposes adapter internals to tests; only use for direct field inspection, not for bypassing the service interface.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory Create/Update/Delete implementations. RegisterPreUpdateMeterHook is a noop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateMeter matches by ID or Key in the in-memory slice — both must match the namespace to avoid cross-namespace mutations.

key_file_guides.role: ListMeters (in-memory filter + pagination) and GetMeterByIDOrSlug + ReplaceMeters (PG sync logic).
key_file_guides.watch_for: PG sync in ReplaceMeters reuses existing (namespace, key) rows; if tests share a template DB, IDs may be remapped silently.

anti_patterns: Using mockadapter in production wiring — it is test-only.
Calling adapter.meters directly from tests instead of going through the Service interface.
Calling SetDBClient after meters are already seeded without calling ReplaceMeters — existing in-memory meters won't be synced to PG.
Expecting RegisterPreUpdateMeterHook to work — it is a noop in the mock.
Using context.Background() in tests when t.Context() is available — breaks test-scoped cancellation.

decisions.decision: Optional PG sync via SetDBClient rather than always requiring a DB.
decisions.rationale: Pure in-memory tests need no DB; only tests that also create features (FK on features.meter_id) need PG sync.

openmeter/meter/service.purpose: Business logic layer for the meter domain. Service delegates reads to the adapter; ManageService adds mutation orchestration including event type validation, cross-domain conflict checks, pre-update hooks, and Watermill event publishing.

patterns.name: Service embeds adapter for read operations
patterns.description: Service struct holds *adapter.Adapter and delegates ListMeters/GetMeterByIDOrSlug directly to it — no additional logic for reads.

patterns.name: ManageService embeds Service + adds mutation orchestration
patterns.description: ManageService embeds meter.Service (the read-only Service), holds *adapter.Adapter separately for mutations, and adds publisher, namespaceManager, eventTypeValidator, preUpdateHooks.
patterns.example: type ManageService struct { meter.Service; hooks *models.ServiceHookRegistry[meter.Meter]; adapter *adapter.Adapter; publisher eventbus.Publisher; ... }

patterns.name: Publish domain events after each successful mutation
patterns.description: After successful Create/Update/Delete, ManageService publishes a typed event (NewMeterCreateEvent, NewMeterUpdateEvent, NewMeterDeleteEvent) via eventbus.Publisher.

patterns.name: Pre-update hook registry
patterns.description: RegisterPreUpdateMeterHook appends hooks to hooks registry; UpdateMeter calls s.hooks.PreUpdate before calling adapter.UpdateMeter, enabling cross-domain validation.
patterns.example: if err := s.hooks.PreUpdate(ctx, &currentMeter); err != nil { return meter.Meter{}, err }

patterns.name: Cross-domain conflict checks before delete
patterns.description: DeleteMeter checks HasActiveFeatureForMeter and HasEntitlementForMeter via adapter; returns GenericConflictError if active references exist.
patterns.example: if hasFeatures { return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("meter has active features and cannot be deleted")) }

key_file_guides.role: ManageService with Create/Update/Delete + hook/event plumbing and all cross-domain validation logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateMeter calls namespaceManager.CreateNamespace as a side effect (workaround); this namespace creation happens after DB write and publishes an event. Publishing events before adapter mutation succeeds is a violation.

key_file_guides.role: Read-only Service struct wrapping adapter; thin delegation layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service is instantiated inside NewManage via New(adapter) — do not instantiate Service separately when ManageService is needed.

anti_patterns: Adding DB queries directly to service.go or manage.go — all persistence goes through the adapter.
Publishing events before the adapter mutation succeeds — events must follow successful writes.
Skipping the pre-update hooks in UpdateMeter — downstream billing/entitlement hooks rely on them.
Adding adapter methods directly to Service interface — Service is read-only; ManageService holds direct *adapter.Adapter reference for cross-domain adapter methods not on the interface.

decisions.decision: Service layer publishes Watermill events after mutations rather than in the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Adapter is a pure persistence layer; event publishing is a business concern that belongs above it to keep the adapter testable without an event bus.

decisions.decision: ManageService holds a direct *adapter.Adapter reference in addition to the embedded meter.Service.
decisions.rationale: Cross-domain checks (HasActiveFeatureForMeter, HasEntitlementForMeter) are adapter methods not exposed on the meter.Service interface, requiring direct adapter access.

code_examples.scenario: Delete a meter with cross-domain conflict guard and post-delete event publish
